name: Dick Wellstood
abstract: Richard MacQueen Wellstood (November 25, 1927 – July 24, 1987) was an American jazz pianist.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: College Jazz Goes to Carnegie Hall
Newport Jazz Festival: Solo Piano I

works.t: Kiss Me, Kate: So In Love

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: Musicians for Each Other
Kool Jazz Festival: Stormy Weather: A Salute to Ethel Waters

about_quotes.speaker: Big Chielf Moore
about_quotes.evidence: your first one was "Big Chief Moore's Pow Wow Jazz Band," with Ed Polger on clarinet, Dick Wellstood on piano

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Locke
about_quotes.evidence: So I got to be really good friends with him, and just watched him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Locke interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York, NY, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: I mentioned before that we had a little trio with Dick Wellstood

about_quotes.speaker: Wild Bill Davison
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Buster Bailey on clarinet and Dick Wellstood on piano and Cliff Leeman on drums and Willie Wayman on bass - who was on trombone? Buster Bailey was on clarinet -- we can't think who was on trombone--- [INTERVIEWEE] Oh, Vic Dickenson---

about_quotes.speaker: John Budd
about_quotes.evidence: Dick Wellstood I got to know him, and also he had a big, he almost bled to death after a tonsillectomy one time and his wife was a nurse and was telling me about that so we talked about music in that case.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Budd interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997 [transcript only]

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: That’s the first time I saw Roy Eldridge, who had Dick Wellstood on piano... These are all people who are not too well known... various piano players, including Dick Wellstood
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
